title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Healthcheck Readiness
summary: A queue worker ignores termination, accumulates zombies, reports healthy while stalled, and can exhaust host memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker healthcheck readiness increment in bounded process lifecycle and health policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 512 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness. A defensible solution applies Docker Healthcheck Readiness, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded process lifecycle and health policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-healthcheck-readiness — Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-4
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker process signals health resources 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An unhealthy container is automatically restarted by Docker in every configuration. This fails the Docker Healthcheck Readiness contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
